code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test02-SEP-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible glomerular damage. Mild hypoglycemia is noted. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but without evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Glucosuria and mild ketonuria are present. Findings are consistent with renal impairment, likely of glomerular origin; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
